Welcome to the team sheets. We are gonna be starting off going over staffing. So this sheet here is going to be pretty important to have. This is your main sheet. And this shows the current positions that you have and who holds those positions and what their hours are and what their pay is. Okay, I took the payoff of here, but you can easily put their pay on just as a reminder for yourself. But this is something that helps you stay really organized as an owner. So this is set for class F to show all of the positions. You simply just would not have all of these positions and you might have longer lists of responsibilities under the owner or the ops manager or whoever you have. But the key is, and this is why I started it off with the class F is that all of these things that are over here, so you can see we have a color coded and it says, they're on the left hand side, it says their priority, their staff, so the staff of who is doing that priority. Their hours and the responsibilities. Even if you don't have a very robust staff, all of these responsibilities still need to happen. You can't just get rid of, because you don't have a retention manager, just get rid of the retention stuff. You will have to do them, just someone else has to do them. So maybe you paste all of these underneath the owner. So this is a simple breakdown of the positions. So here we have under the blue one is going to be trainers and lead trainer. The orange is going to be the ops manager. You can do whatever colors you want. The purple is retention manager, then we have the challenge manager, then we have the sales manager, then we have the front desk and we have the owner. So this is all stuff that needs to happen at the gym and it's basically just breaking it up between who's doing it, what their hours are, so you have a really good understanding of all the tasks. I mean, some of these things, maybe you need to start doing them, because there's not really any in here that I would feel comfortable like cutting out and they just don't happen. So take a look and this might be a good, maybe highlight all the ones that you're not doing and figure out how you can have that assigned to somebody. So again, it has the priority, the actual staff member that's doing that, the hours that they're holding, how many hours per week, their daily responsibilities and as you scroll down, we have their mantra, we have the four hours document, so you can easily access it. Just put a link to your four hours document and then the training portal. So once you start doing your training portals, those links will pop up in here as well. And so if you need to quickly access the four hours or the training portal, it's all right there. So this, it might start off with only three positions, but that's an incentive to grow it and put in whatever you feel fits best for your gym.
